prompt: A photorealistic, eye-level shot of a public park. Instead of a normal straight sidewalk, the concrete path zigzags wildly and erratically in sharp, chaotic angles just to perfectly avoid every single tiny fallen leaf, pebble, and twig on the grass. The path is completely absurd and unusable for a normal walker because it over-accommodates every tiny piece of debris. Natural daylight, highly detailed, shot on a 35mm lens. Absolutely no text, no diagrams, no glowing lines, and no UI elements.
tradition_approach: I targeted the flat, non-perspectival composition, the heavy ink outlining, the use of gold leaf, and the specific branching structure of 'Tree of Jesse' illustrations to represent the algorithmic splitting of data.
